evals.prompt: Create a Drupal 10 module called `user_activity` that provides: 1) A custom service that retrieves activity data for a given user (number of nodes they authored, last access timestamp). 2) A page at `/user/{user}/activity` displaying the user's activity summary with a dynamic page title showing their display name. 3) A JSON endpoint at `/api/user/{user}/activity` returning the activity data as JSON. 4) Proper access control on both routes. 5) Use dependency injection. D11 compatible.
evals.expected_output: Custom service in .services.yml, controller with create() DI, {user} entity auto-upcasting, _title_callback for dynamic title, _permission access
expectations: A .services.yml file exists that registers a custom service with `arguments:` containing at least one @-prefixed core service reference (e.g., @entity_type.manager)
Route parameter is named `{user}` (matching the entity type machine name) for automatic entity upcasting -- the controller does NOT manually call User::load() or \Drupal\user\Entity\User::load()
Route file defines `_title_callback` pointing to a controller method for dynamic page title (not just a static `_title` string)
Controller injects the custom service via create() factory method -- the service is pulled from the container by its module-prefixed name (e.g., user_activity.something)
The custom service class does NOT extend ControllerBase -- it is a standalone class with constructor injection via .services.yml arguments
Routes use `_permission` for access control (not `_access: 'TRUE'` which bypasses all access checking)
The module enables successfully: ddev drush en user_activity -y returns exit code 0
E2E: curl to /user/1/activity as authenticated admin returns HTTP 200 with page content (not 404 or 403)
